ddls:
CREATE TABLE "municipalities" (
  "municipality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each municipality */,
  "tier" TEXT /* Tier of the municipality (e.g., LT, UT) */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the municipality */,
  "stats_can_population_2001" INTEGER /* Population of the municipality according to Statistics Canada (2001) */,
  "interest_payout" REAL /* Interest payout for the municipality */,
  "province_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the provinces table */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the regions table */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the municipality */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the municipality */,
  "area_km2" REAL /* Area of the municipality in square kilometers */,
  "population_density" REAL /* Population density of the municipality */,
  "growth_rate" REAL /* Population growth rate of the municipality */,
  "unemployment_rate" REAL /* Unemployment rate of the municipality */,
  "avg_household_income" REAL /* Average household income of the municipality */,
  "median_house_price" REAL /* Median house price of the municipality */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("municipality_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "provinces" (
  "province_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each province */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the province */,
  "stats_can_population_2001" INTEGER /* Population of the province according to Statistics Canada (2001) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("province_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "province_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the provinces table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographic_trends" (
  "demographic_trend_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic trend */,
  "municipality_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the municipalities table */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the demographic data */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the municipality for the given year */,
  "growth_rate" REAL /* Population growth rate of the municipality for the given year */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_trend_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "economic_indicators" (
  "economic_indicator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each economic indicator */,
  "municipality_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the municipalities table */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the economic data */,
  "interest_payout" REAL /* Interest payout for the municipality for the given year */,
  "unemployment_rate" REAL /* Unemployment rate of the municipality for the given year */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("economic_indicator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shared_services" (
  "shared_service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each shared service */,
  "municipality_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the municipalities table */,
  "partner_municipality_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the municipalities table to establish partnerships between municipalities */,
  "service_type" TEXT /* Type of shared service (e.g., transportation, healthcare) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("shared_service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regional_partnerships" (
  "regional_partnership_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each regional partnership */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the regions table */,
  "municipality_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the municipalities table */,
  "partnership_type" TEXT /* Type of regional partnership (e.g., economic development, environmental conservation) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("regional_partnership_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., administrator, analyst) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user account */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "municipality_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the municipalities table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the users table */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the roles table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., Statistics Canada) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_source_id")
)
